message:
feat(python): numpy.linalg det / inv wrapper parity (franken_numpy-m733)

Add fnp_python.det(a) and fnp_python.inv(a) python wrappers.
Real 2-D square inputs route to fnp_linalg::det_nxn /
fnp_linalg::inv_nxn respectively for a zero-overhead Rust fast
path; complex, batched (..., M, M), or non-2-D shapes passthrough
to numpy.linalg so numpy's broadcasting and complex semantics are
preserved exactly.

Covered by one pyo3 oracle test exercising both entry points
across six scenarios each: 2x2 real invertible, 3x3 real
invertible, 4x4 identity (det=1, inv=I), batched stack of three
2x2 matrices, and a complex128 2x2 — every scenario compared via
numpy.isclose / numpy.allclose against np.linalg.{det,inv}.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
